summary: SEAIPC 2026 new features + full regression tested. 45/46 backend tests pass (12/12 new SEAIPC tests pass: forgot-password, reset-password, DOI on publish, inline PDF preview). One pre-existing flaky test (test_author_uploads_revision) failed due to intermittent Emergent object storage 500 upstream (not SEAIPC regression). Frontend smoke tested: SEAIPC rebranding, /call-for-papers renders 39 sub-themes, /dates (6 date rows), /templates (EN + MY .docx download buttons), /forgot-password + /reset-password pages, Login has working 'Forgot password?' link, /about shows SEAIPC Waqf content, PaperDetail Preview iframe modal works for editor, Decision dialog has conditional DOI input (decision-doi testid) when 'publish' selected.

minor.issue: Intermittent 500 from upstream Emergent Object Storage (https://integrations.emergentagent.com/objstore) - caused 1 flaky failure in full regression run. Not a code bug. Test has skip-on-storage-500 guard but this one passed the initial 200 check then upstream failed on a subsequent upload.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing aria-describedby (a11y warning, not blocking)

action_items: (Optional) Add aria-describedby to PaperDetail Dialog components to silence Radix warning
(Optional) Split server.py (700 lines) into routers if growth continues - carried over from iteration_1
(Optional) Tighten CORS allow_origins from '*' to explicit list now that SEAIPC domain is known (seaipc2026.imz.or.id)
critical_code_review_comments: preview_file endpoint accepts JWT via query param which will be logged in access logs / server logs - consider signed short-lived tokens (HMAC with short TTL) specifically scoped to file_id to reduce blast radius if the URL is shared or logged.

Password reset token stored in db.password_reset_tokens with 1-hour expiry but no index on 'token' field or TTL index - consider adding a TTL index on expires_at for automatic cleanup and a unique index on token.
forgot-password has no per-email rate limit - attacker can flood resend attempts. Consider throttling to N requests per email per hour.

DOI auto-generation uses first 8 chars of paper UUID (f"10.9999/seaipc2026.{paper_id[:8]}") - collision-free but not ISO 26324 compliant (10.9999 is reserved for testing only). Fine for dev but document this for production registration with DOI registrar.

Publish decision overwrites doi on every publish call - if an editor re-publishes after correction, custom DOI from previous call is wiped. Consider preserving existing DOI when body.doi not provided.

Templates page uses <a download> approach which may still open in new tab rather than force-download depending on Content-Disposition of the CDN hosted .docx. Verify CDN sets attachment disposition; otherwise consider adding a server-side proxy endpoint.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_seaipc_features.py (new - 12 tests for password reset, DOI on publish, file preview)

success_rate.backend: 97.8% (45/46) - 100% on SEAIPC new-feature tests (12/12). 1 flaky storage upstream failure.
success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke-tested new routes + rebrand + preview/decision dialogs

test_credentials: admin@ojs.com/admin123, editor@ojs.com/editor123, reviewer@ojs.com/reviewer123, author@ojs.com/author123 - all verified
seed_data_creation: TEST_-prefixed papers (TEST_DOI_Auto_*, TEST_DOI_Explicit_*, TEST_Preview_*) created via author and published via editor. Temporary users test_reset_* created for full password-reset flow and cleaned up by admin delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two backend test files now: /app/backend/tests/test_ojs_api.py (34 original) + /app/backend/tests/test_seaipc_features.py (12 SEAIPC). Run both: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=... pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Password-reset tests extract the reset token by tailing /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log grep '[PASSWORD RESET]' - depends on logger.info being sent to stderr (currently works). Email sending is MOCKED (RESEND_API_KEY empty). Flaky test to watch: test_author_uploads_revision - retry on storage 500. Preview endpoint supports both ?token=<jwt> query param (for iframe) and Authorization Bearer header; enforces the same role-based authz as /download (admin/editor/author-owner/assigned-reviewer).
rca of the issue: No SEAIPC regression bugs found. The only failing test (test_author_uploads_revision) is due to intermittent upstream 500 from Emergent Object Storage - same test passed in iteration_1 and passes on retry. Reproduction: retry the test; mitigation: add exponential-backoff retry in upload_paper_file wrapper OR rely on storage guard skip already present in test.
